metadata.description: 6-module certification teaching reps to understand, position, and sell the Informativ Payments channel partnership product. Covers the payments engine, partner value propositions, use cases by platform type, competitive differentiation vs. legacy players, channel pitch execution, and AI discoverability strategy. This certification is specifically about the Informativ Payments API product sold to software partners — NOT SmartPencil, which is Informativ's dealer-facing desking tool.
metadata.target_audience: Sales representatives and channel partnership team members
metadata.important_distinction: Informativ Payments is Informativ's channel partnership API product enabling software partners to embed credit-qualified payments into their platforms. SmartPencil is Informativ's dealer-facing first-pencil desking tool used by desk managers, F&I, and sales teams inside the dealership. Both use Informativ's payment calculation engine, but they serve different buyers (partners vs. dealers), different use cases (API integration vs. in-store deal structuring), and different sales motions (channel/partner sales vs. direct dealer sales).

modules.title: What Is Informativ Payments — Product Foundation
modules.description: Understand what Informativ Payments is, who it's built for, and how it fits within Informativ's broader platform. Establish the critical distinction between Informativ Payments (channel API for software partners) and SmartPencil (dealer-facing desking tool).
key_concepts: Informativ Payments is a channel partnership product — a REST API that enables software partners to embed real, credit-qualified, VIN-level monthly payments into their platforms
The buyer is not the dealer — the buyer is the technology partner (CRM, marketplace, ad tech, AI chat, digital retailing platform). Dealers benefit indirectly through the partner's enhanced platform
The payments engine calculates real, fundable monthly payments using live lender programs, OEM incentives, regional taxes/fees, and dealer pricing rules — not estimates
Informativ Payments extends Informativ's trusted credit and compliance infrastructure into the payments layer of automotive commerce
SmartPencil is NOT Informativ Payments — SmartPencil is the dealer-facing first-pencil desking tool used by desk managers, F&I, and sales teams inside the dealership to structure deals
Both products use Informativ's payment calculation engine, but Informativ Payments is an API for partners while SmartPencil is an in-store tool for dealer employees
Informativ powers thousands of dealerships and processes millions of credit transactions annually — this scale and trust is what underpins Payments
Usage-based pricing (VIN-based) — partners pay only for VINs they need, no flat rooftop fees, no setup costs, no bloated minimums

questions.question: What is Informativ Payments?

options.A: A dealer-facing desking tool for structuring car deals in the showroom
options.B: A REST API that enables software partners to embed real, credit-qualified payments into their platforms
options.C: A credit report provider for automotive dealerships
options.D: A consumer-facing car payment calculator website

questions.question: Who is the primary buyer for Informativ Payments?

options.A: Dealership GMs and desk managers
options.B: Car shoppers looking for payment estimates
options.C: Technology partners — CRMs, marketplaces, ad tech, AI chat, and digital retailing platforms
options.D: Credit bureaus and lenders

questions.question: What is the key distinction between Informativ Payments and SmartPencil?

options.A: SmartPencil is for new vehicles only; Payments is for used vehicles
options.B: SmartPencil is Informativ's dealer-facing desking tool; Payments is the channel API for software partners
options.C: Payments calculates lease payments; SmartPencil calculates loan payments
options.D: They are the same product with different names

questions.question: True or False: Informativ Payments returns estimated payments based on generic rate tables.

options.A: True
options.B: False

questions.question: How is Informativ Payments priced for channel partners?

options.A: Flat monthly rooftop fee per dealer
options.B: Annual enterprise license with setup costs
options.C: Usage-based pricing — partners pay only for VINs they need payments for
options.D: Free for partners; dealers pay a per-transaction fee

questions.question: What foundation gives Informativ Payments its credibility in the market?

options.A: Informativ's background in vehicle history reports
options.B: Informativ's trusted credit, compliance, and customer insight infrastructure powering thousands of dealerships
options.C: A partnership with a single large OEM
options.D: Informativ's consumer marketing platform

options.A: A credit score and recommended vehicle
options.B: A list of nearby dealerships
options.C: Real, credit-qualified monthly payments for loan and lease scenarios in milliseconds
options.D: An estimated MSRP and invoice price

questions.question: Which of the following is TRUE about Informativ Payments vs. SmartPencil?

options.A: Both are sold directly to dealership employees for in-store use
options.B: Both serve different buyers — Payments serves software partners; SmartPencil serves dealer desk managers and F&I teams
options.C: SmartPencil is an API product and Payments is a desktop application
options.D: Informativ Payments replaced SmartPencil in 2026

modules.title: The Problem We Solve — Affordability and the Expectation Gap
modules.description: Understand the market problem that makes Informativ Payments urgent: the expectation gap between online estimated payments and in-store reality, and why affordability has become the #1 barrier in automotive commerce.
key_concepts: The average new car is over $50,000. Interest rates are up, loan terms are longer, and affordability is the biggest barrier to closing deals
Shoppers want to know what they can actually afford before clicking 'contact dealer' — most platforms are built around price, not payments
The expectation gap: a customer sees a $600 monthly estimate online and finds out it's $790 in-store — that gap kills trust, kills deals, and kills the digital journey
Informativ Payments replaces 'estimated' with 'qualified' — showing real, fundable payments from actual lender programs, real incentives, and live dealer pricing
For partners, this is not just a feature — it's the difference between a platform shoppers trust and one they abandon
Partners who don't offer real payments leave dealers vulnerable to competitors who do
Credit-qualified means the payment is calculated based on the consumer's actual credit profile via soft pull — no impact to the consumer's credit score
The gap between online and in-store creates re-desks, longer time at the desk, lost deals, and eroded consumer trust

questions.question: What is the 'expectation gap' that Informativ Payments solves?

options.A: The difference between MSRP and invoice price
options.B: The disconnect between estimated online payments and the actual qualified payment a customer faces in-store
options.C: The time between when a customer applies for credit and gets approved
options.D: The gap between new and used vehicle pricing

questions.question: Why has affordability become the biggest barrier in automotive commerce?

options.A: Dealers have stopped offering financing
options.B: Vehicle inventory has decreased significantly
options.C: The average new car exceeds $50,000, interest rates are up, and loan terms are longer
options.D: Consumers prefer to pay cash

options.A: The dealer makes more profit on the deal
options.B: Trust is broken, deals are lost, and the digital journey fails
options.C: The customer is pleasantly surprised
options.D: The lender adjusts the rate automatically

options.A: The payment uses a generic credit score range estimate
options.B: The payment is calculated using the consumer's actual credit profile via soft pull with no impact to their credit score
options.C: The consumer must submit a full credit application
options.D: The payment only works for consumers with excellent credit

questions.question: True or False: Most dealer websites, CRMs, and digital retail tools today are built around real, fundable payments.

options.A: True
options.B: False

questions.question: What does Informativ Payments replace in the shopping experience?

options.A: The need for a credit check entirely
options.B: Estimated or generic payment numbers with real, credit-qualified, fundable payments

questions.question: Why should a software partner care about the expectation gap?

options.A: It doesn't affect software partners — only dealers
options.B: Partners who don't offer real payments leave their dealers vulnerable and risk losing platform trust and engagement
options.C: The expectation gap only matters for luxury vehicles
options.D: Consumers don't notice payment differences

modules.title: Core Value Propositions and Partner Talk Tracks
modules.description: Master the 7 core value propositions that differentiate Informativ Payments. Learn how to articulate each prop in a partner-facing conversation.
key_concepts: Value Prop 1 — Estimated to Qualified: Replace estimated payments that fall apart in-store with real, credit-qualified, transactable payments calculated the way a dealership desks a deal

Value Prop 2 — Best Payment, Not Just Penny-Perfect: Legacy systems produce penny-perfect math. Informativ optimizes for the best payment — blending incentives, lender programs, and dealer margin to produce lower payments for shoppers and higher gross for dealers
Value Prop 3 — Built for Software Companies: Cloud-native from day one. Low latency, modern architecture, clean documentation. Not a legacy desking system retrofitted into an API
Value Prop 4 — Usage-Based Pricing: Partners pay only for VINs they push payments to. No blanket rooftop fee. More affordable for dealers, more flexible to package inside partner platforms

Value Prop 5 — Compliance as a Core Capability: Powered by Informativ's credit, identity, and compliance backbone. Every payment meets lender, OEM, and regulatory requirements. Enterprise-ready safety with no added engineering or legal overhead
Value Prop 6 — Payments Everywhere: Plugs into every touchpoint — lead forms, AI chat, ad platforms, online desking, CRMs, marketplaces. Credit-qualified payments across the entire shopper journey

Value Prop 7 — Speed, Freshness, and Reliability: Engine updates incentives, taxes, lender programs, and dealer rules in near real-time. Responds in milliseconds. No stale data, no caching delays, no performance drag

The Outcome: A modern, API-driven payment experience that builds trust, raises dealer profitability, improves conversion, reduces surprises and re-desks, and differentiates the partner's platform instantly

questions.question: What does Value Prop 2 ('More Than Penny-Perfect') mean?

options.A: Informativ rounds payments to the nearest penny
options.B: Informativ optimizes for the best payment by blending incentives, lender programs, and dealer margin — aligning lower payments for shoppers with higher gross for dealers
options.C: Informativ calculates payments faster than competitors
options.D: Informativ provides more decimal places in payment calculations

questions.question: How is Informativ Payments different from legacy payment providers architecturally?

options.A: Informativ is a desktop application
options.B: Informativ retrofitted a desking system into an API, similar to competitors
options.C: Informativ Payments is cloud-native from day one — low latency, modern architecture, clean documentation for product teams
options.D: Informativ uses the same technology stack as OfferLogix

questions.question: Why is usage-based pricing attractive to partners and their dealers?

options.A: It costs more but provides better data
options.B: Partners only pay for VINs they need payments for — no blanket rooftop fees, more affordable for dealers, more flexible for packaging
options.C: It requires a multi-year contract commitment
options.D: Pricing is fixed regardless of volume

options.A: Partners must build their own compliance layer
options.B: Every payment meets lender, OEM, and regulatory requirements out of the box — partners get enterprise-ready safety without added engineering or legal overhead
options.C: Compliance only applies to credit reports, not payments
options.D: Partners need to hire compliance officers to use the API

questions.question: Which Value Prop addresses the fact that Informativ Payments works across ads, websites, chat, CRM, and digital retailing?

options.A: Value Prop 2 — Best Payment
options.B: Value Prop 4 — Usage-Based Pricing
options.C: Value Prop 6 — Payments Everywhere
options.D: Value Prop 7 — Speed and Freshness

questions.question: What does 'Speed, Freshness, and Reliability' specifically refer to?

options.A: The speed of partner onboarding
options.B: The engine updates incentives, taxes, lender programs, and dealer rules in near real-time and responds in milliseconds with no stale data or caching delays
options.C: The frequency of software updates to the API
options.D: The speed of customer credit approvals

options.A: Value Prop 4 — Usage-Based Pricing
options.B: Value Prop 1 — Estimated to Qualified: real, credit-qualified payments calculated the way a dealership desks a deal, not estimates that fall apart in-store
options.C: Value Prop 7 — Speed and Freshness
options.D: Value Prop 3 — Built for Software Companies

questions.question: What is the combined outcome Informativ Payments delivers for a partner's platform?

options.A: Lower technology costs and fewer API calls
options.B: Builds trust with shoppers, raises dealer profitability, improves conversion, reduces re-desks, and differentiates the partner's platform
options.C: Replaces the partner's existing CRM functionality
options.D: Eliminates the need for dealer inventory management

modules.title: Use Cases by Platform Type — Where Payments Fits
modules.description: Learn how to position Informativ Payments for each partner segment: AdTech/MarTech, dealer websites/marketplaces, AI chat, CRM/BDC, desking/digital retailing, and lead gen/equity mining.
key_concepts: AdTech/MarTech/Search: Payment-qualified ads that target shoppers based on affordability. Dynamic ads updating in real time. Higher CTR, lower CPA, better-quality traffic with one API call

Dealer Websites/Marketplaces: Real shop-by-payment experience. VIN-specific, credit-qualified payments via soft pull boost VDP engagement and lead quality. Shoppers bounce when site payments don't match reality

AI Chat/Messaging: Solves the 'final mile' problem — even LLMs like OpenAI and Anthropic break when asked about vehicle monthly payments. Informativ's APIs return fundable payments in-chat via soft pull, turning the chatbot into a transaction-ready experience
CRM/BDC/Sales Tools: BDC agents and sales teams lead follow-ups with real payment offers instead of generic outreach. CRM texts and emails powered by fundable payments
Desking/Digital Retailing: Fundable, credit-qualified payments inside workflows reduce re-desks, improve F&I handoff, and protect dealer margins. Online payments match in-store
Lead Gen/Nurture/CDP/Equity Mining: First-response teams, CDPs, and equity mining platforms embed real payment context into every touchpoint across the customer lifecycle
Full Funnel Coverage: Marketing → Lead Gen → Nurture → Sales Process → Deal. Informativ Payments covers the entire automotive commerce funnel
Each use case has its own pitch template and LinkedIn DM template — personalize the outreach based on the partner's platform type

questions.question: For an AdTech/MarTech partner, what is the primary value of Informativ Payments?

options.A: Replacing the partner's ad serving platform
options.B: Payment-qualified ads that drive higher CTR, lower CPA, and better-quality traffic by showing what shoppers can actually afford
options.C: Providing vehicle inventory data for ad targeting
options.D: Managing the partner's advertising budget

options.C: It eliminates the need for F&I managers

options.A: True
options.B: False — it covers the entire funnel from Marketing to Lead Gen to Nurture to Sales Process to Deal

options.B: Are those estimated payments or real, credit-qualified payments? Shoppers bounce when the numbers don't match what they actually qualify for. Informativ returns VIN-specific, credit-qualified payments via soft pull
options.C: You should remove your current payments and use only ours
options.D: Estimated payments are just as effective as qualified payments

questions.question: Which segment did Informativ identify as having an especially acute 'final mile' problem?

options.A: DMS providers
options.B: AI Chat and Messaging platforms — where even major LLMs cannot answer vehicle payment questions
options.C: Print advertising
options.D: Vehicle auction platforms

modules.title: Competitive Positioning — Legacy Players and Differentiation
modules.description: Understand how Informativ Payments competes against legacy payment providers (OfferLogix, MarketScan) and how to position Informativ's technical and economic advantages in partner conversations.
key_concepts: Legacy players: OfferLogix and MarketScan were solid for their time but are expensive, slow, and disconnected from real credit workflows
OfferLogix/MarketScan were built on older stacks — retrofitted desking systems pushed into APIs. Informativ Payments is cloud-native from day one
Informativ is modern, faster, more accurate, and far more cost-effective than legacy payment engines
Key differentiator: Informativ Payments is built on real credit and compliance infrastructure, not retrofitted old desking systems
Technical advantages: REST APIs, millisecond response times, real-time lender/incentive updates, no stale data, easy integration (days not weeks)
Economic advantage: Usage-based VIN pricing vs. expensive flat fees. No setup costs. No bloated minimums. More affordable for dealers, more flexible for partners
Compliance advantage: Every payment meets lender, OEM, and regulatory requirements — powered by the same backbone used by thousands of dealers

The NADA 30-second talk track: Historically payments meant OfferLogix or MarketScan. Informativ already powers credit and compliance for the industry, and now brings that same accuracy into payments — modern, faster, more accurate, and far more cost-effective

questions.question: Who are the legacy payment competitors in the automotive space?

options.A: Stripe and Square
options.B: OfferLogix and MarketScan
options.C: 700Credit and NCC
options.D: AutoFi and Upstart

questions.question: What is the primary architectural weakness of legacy payment providers?

options.A: They don't support lease calculations
options.B: They were built on older stacks — retrofitted desking systems pushed into APIs — making them slow, expensive, and disconnected from real credit workflows
options.C: They only work with one lender
options.D: They don't have any dealer customers

questions.question: How does Informativ's pricing model compare to legacy providers?

options.A: Informativ is more expensive but higher quality
options.B: Both use the same flat rooftop fee model
options.C: Informativ uses usage-based VIN pricing with no setup costs or bloated minimums — more affordable and more flexible than legacy flat fees
options.D: Informativ charges per credit pull; legacy charges per payment

questions.question: What gives Informativ a compliance advantage over legacy payment providers?

options.A: Informativ has more lawyers on staff
options.B: Every payment is built on Informativ's credit, identity, and compliance backbone already used by thousands of dealers — compliance is core, not bolted on
options.C: Legacy providers don't offer any compliance features
options.D: Informativ requires partners to handle their own compliance

questions.question: A partner says they're currently using OfferLogix. What's your opening positioning?

options.A: OfferLogix is terrible and you should switch immediately
options.B: OfferLogix was solid for its time, but it was built on an older stack. Informativ Payments is cloud-native, modern, faster, more accurate, and far more cost-effective — with usage-based pricing and real credit infrastructure behind it
options.C: We can't compete with OfferLogix on pricing
options.D: OfferLogix and Informativ Payments are essentially the same product

questions.question: True or False: Most partners can integrate Informativ Payments in days, not weeks.

options.A: True — REST APIs, modern architecture, and clean documentation enable fast integration
options.B: False — integration typically takes 3-6 months

questions.question: What is the NADA 30-second competitive positioning statement?

options.A: Informativ Payments is cheaper than everyone else
options.B: Historically payments meant OfferLogix or MarketScan — solid for their time but expensive, slow, and disconnected from real credit workflows. Informativ brings its trusted credit and compliance accuracy into payments — modern, faster, more accurate, and far more cost-effective
options.C: We don't have any competitors in the payments space
options.D: Informativ Payments only works with Informativ's existing dealer customers

modules.title: Channel Pitch Execution and AI Discoverability Strategy
modules.description: Learn how to execute channel partner pitches by platform type, leverage LinkedIn DM templates, and position the AI discoverability / GEO strategy as a forward-looking differentiator for partners.
key_concepts: Each platform type (AdTech, Websites/Marketplaces, AI/Chat, Desking/DR) has a specific pitch template — personalize it to the partner's platform name and current capabilities
LinkedIn DM templates: Short, use-case-specific messages that open doors. Lead with the partner's pain point, not Informativ's features
NADA talk tracks: 15-second intro, 30-second competitive positioning, 1-minute full pitch. Practice all three

AI Discoverability (GEO/AEO) strategy: Car buyers are shifting from Google to AI assistants (ChatGPT, Perplexity, Google AI Mode). Dealers without structured, machine-readable payment data won't be cited by AI agents
Market signals: 25% drop in traditional search by 2026 (Gartner), 4.4x higher conversion from AI search (SEMRush), 93% zero-click rate in Google AI Mode, 50% of searches via AI assistants by 2028
Structured payment data passes the AI citation test: specific, verifiable, locally-grounded, and fresh. Generic content fails all four tests
The AI discoverability flywheel compounds — platforms that establish structured payment data early build citation dominance that late entrants cannot replicate
For every platform type, Informativ Payments creates AI-discoverable dealer content: payment-enriched VDPs, geo-targeted pages, credit-tier guides, and incentive explainers
CTA for all outreach: informativ.com/ub/payments-api/

questions.question: When pitching a website/marketplace partner via LinkedIn DM, what should you lead with?

options.A: Informativ's company history and size
options.B: The partner's pain point — shoppers bounce when site payments don't match what they qualify for
options.C: Informativ's pricing and contract terms
options.D: A list of all Informativ's products

questions.question: What does GEO (Generative Engine Optimization) mean for automotive platforms?

options.A: Optimizing for geographic search results on Google Maps
options.B: A new SEO technique focused on keyword density
options.C: Earning citations inside AI-generated answers — the currency is no longer a click but a citation in an AI response
options.D: A social media marketing strategy

questions.question: According to Gartner, what percentage of searches will involve an AI assistant by 2028?
Why does structured payment data pass the AI citation test while generic content fails?

options.A: AI only reads payment data and ignores other content
options.B: Structured payment data is specific, verifiable, locally-grounded, and fresh — all four attributes AI agents prioritize for citations
options.C: Payment data is always ranked higher by Google's algorithm
options.D: Generic content is blocked by AI search engines

questions.question: What is the AI discoverability 'flywheel effect'?

options.A: AI search costs decrease over time
options.B: Platforms that establish structured payment data early build citation dominance that compounds and cannot be easily replicated by late entrants
options.C: AI agents rotate between different data sources each month
options.D: All platforms receive equal AI citation authority regardless of timing

questions.question: A partner asks: 'Why should I care about AI search? My dealers rank well on Google.' What's your response?

options.A: You're right — Google rankings are all that matter
options.B: Gartner predicts a 25% drop in traditional search by 2026. Buyers are shifting to AI assistants. The 93% zero-click rate in Google AI Mode means even Google is becoming AI-first. Dealers without structured payment data won't be cited
options.C: AI search doesn't affect the automotive industry
options.D: You should stop investing in SEO entirely

questions.question: When delivering the NADA 15-second intro, what should you always mention?

options.A: SmartPencil and all four package tiers
options.B: That Informativ is a tech software company providing credit and compliance for over 5,000 dealerships, and you're introducing the new payments product
options.C: Informativ's stock price and revenue figures
options.D: The names of all legacy competitors
